title: Chapter 2368: The Realm of Absolute God
content:
Chapter 2368: The Realm of Absolute God

"Who am I...?" Yuan turned to look at the True God after killing Mov. "I’m Yuan. As for the power I used, it was Demon Sealing Aura."

"Demon Sealing Aura? You were a Sealer this entire time?" The True God frowned when he realized that Yuan had been going easy on him this entire time.

Had Yuan used Demon Sealing Aura from the start to suppress him, he wouldn’t have been able to fight with his full capability. However, there was another reason he felt upset.

"I don’t mean your Demon Sealing Aura," he said. "That power you displayed to kill Mov... that was something entirely different!"

Yuan shrugged, pretending he had no idea what the True God was talking about.

In truth, he had briefly unleashed his Eternal Essence, overwhelming Mov instantly and killing him before he could even comprehend what had happened.

This was also Yuan’s first time fusing his Eternal Essence with his Demon Sealing Aura, and unsurprisingly, the results were astonishing.

"Want to continue?" Yuan asked the True God a moment later.

"..."

The True God took a deep breath before shaking his head.

"I’m no longer in the mood, especially after realizing you’ve been going easy on me. I still can’t believe a first-level False God was holding back against me."

"Why did you attack me in the first place?" Yuan asked suddenly. "Were you really that bothered by my presence?"

The True God shook his head. "No. At first, I believed you had wandered here by mistake, so I wanted to drive you away. This place is too dangerous for a False God, after all."

He continued, "But after fighting you, I realized I had underestimated you. Now that I know you can even kill True Gods, there’s no reason for me to chase you away anymore."

Yuan raised an eyebrow at the explanation. According to him, the True God had actually been trying to help, which was truly unexpected.

"Why were you exiled to the Deprived Crimson Valley?" Yuan asked, seemingly interested in the True God’s background.

"My reason for being exiled...?" The True God fell silent for a moment before responding, "To be frank, I have long forgotten the reason. I wasn’t even a False God when I was exiled over fifty billion years ago."

"Have you ever thought about leaving?" Yuan asked.

"Many times," the True God answered. "But escaping this place with my own strength is impossible—even for a True God."

He continued in a low voice, "And even if I did manage to escape, it wouldn’t matter. We exiles carry a brand etched into our souls. No matter where we go or what disguise we wear, we will always be recognized and hunted."

The exiles of the Deprived Crimson Valley were much like the exiles of the Primordial Realm. Even if they somehow managed to escape, their branding ensured they could never truly run from their fate.

"Enough questions about me. What are you doing in the Eighth Valley? Are you just looking to fight True Gods?"

"My destination isn’t the Eighth Valley, but the Final Valley," Yuan calmly replied.

"The Final Valley?! Are you out of your mind?!" The True God exclaimed in shock after hearing this.

"Forget about the Final Valley, even the Ninth Valley is too dangerous these days! Why the hell would you want to go there?"

"I have some business with Supreme Ruler Dena, that’s all."

"Supreme Ruler Dena?!" The True God nearly turned and ran away the moment he heard her name.

"What’s going on with the Ninth Valley?" Yuan ignored his shock and asked.

"I don’t know how to explain it. It’s better if you witnessed it yourself. Because of what’s happening in the Ninth Valley, all of the True Gods and Absolute Gods dwelling there have moved to the Eighth Valley."

"Wait... Absolute Gods?" Yuan raised an eyebrow at this new terminology. "What are those?"

The True God looked at Yuan with a puzzled face.

"You... don’t know about Absolute Gods?" he had to confirm what he heard.

Yuan calmly nodded before giving a random excuse, "I was in seclusion for a long time."

"How long were you in seclusion for? The realm of Absolute God was discovered over ten billion years ago! It’s basically what’s above the True God Realm, and only about twenty individuals have managed to reach that level even after its discovery ten billion years ago."

’A realm above Cultivation God, huh?’ Yuan thought to himself.

While the Cultivation God was considered the peak of cultivation in the Nine Heavens, the path of cultivation itself was infinite. There was no true peak—only new realms waiting to be discovered with time.

With how much time passed in the Demonic Realm compared to the Nine Heavens, it was no surprise that they had managed to discover what’s above Cultivation God.

The Demonic Realm being ahead of the Nine Heavens wasn’t anything new to Yuan, as there were already hundreds of Cultivation Gods when he visited as Tian Chenyu, while the Nine Heavens had only discovered its existence.

"An Absolute God... I’d like to meet and fight one," Yuan remarked with a smile on his face.

"If you have a death wish, go ahead and fight one. They’ll kill you before you can even register what happened," said the True God.

"Are there any in the Deprived Crimson Valley?" Yuan asked.

"There are... two of them."

"If they’re really that powerful, why are they afraid of the Ninth Valley?"

"It’s not that they’re scared. They just don’t want to deal with it since it’s a hassle."

"Now I really want to see what’s at the Ninth Valley. Thanks for the information. I’ll be taking my leave now."

Yuan then called out, "Come out, Lev! We’re moving again!"

A few moments later, Lev emerged from a hole he’d dug in the ground and slowly approached them with his head lowered, as if he were too afraid to even look at the True God.
